en: It is not enough to be busy. So are the ants. The question is: What are we busy about?
zh: 忙碌是不够的，蚂蚁也很忙。问题是：我们在忙什么？
author: Henry David Thoreau

en: Take rest; a field that has rested gives a bountiful crop.
zh: 休息吧；休耕的土地才能长出丰盛的庄稼。
author: Ovid

en: The bow kept forever taut will break.
zh: 弓弦长绷必断。
author: Phaedrus

en: In the midst of movement and chaos, keep stillness inside of you.
zh: 在纷繁的运动与混乱之中，保持内心的宁静。
author: Deepak Chopra

en: To keep the body in good health is a duty, otherwise we shall not be able to keep our mind strong and clear.
zh: 保持身体健康是一种责任，否则我们无法让心智强健而清明。
author: Buddha

en: Rest is not idleness, and to lie sometimes on the grass under trees on a summer's day, listening to the murmur of the water, or watching the clouds float across the sky, is by no means a waste of time.
zh: 休息不是懒惰。在夏日里偶尔躺在树下的草地上，听流水低吟，看云朵飘过天空，绝非虚度光阴。
author: John Lubbock

en: Almost everything will work again if you unplug it for a few minutes, including you.
zh: 几乎所有东西拔掉电源几分钟后都能恢复运转，包括你自己。
author: Anne Lamott

en: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
zh: 自然从不匆忙，却万事皆成。
author: Lao Tzu

en: The greatest weapon against stress is our ability to choose one thought over another.
zh: 对抗压力最强大的武器，是我们在一个念头与另一个念头之间做选择的能力。
author: William James

en: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
zh: 追随自然的步伐吧：她的秘密是耐心。
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

en: The time to relax is when you don't have time for it.
zh: 最该放松的时候，恰恰是你觉得没时间放松的时候。
author: Sydney J. Harris

en: Tension is who you think you should be. Relaxation is who you are.
zh: 紧张来自你以为自己该成为的样子，放松才是你本来的样子。
author: Chinese Proverb

en: Your body is a temple, but only if you treat it as one.
zh: 你的身体是一座殿堂，但前提是你把它当作殿堂对待。
author: Astrid Alauda

en: He who has health has hope, and he who has hope has everything.
zh: 有健康就有希望，有希望就拥有一切。
author: Thomas Carlyle

en: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom. Mastering others is strength; mastering yourself is true power.
zh: 知人者智，自知者明。胜人者有力，自胜者强。
author: Lao Tzu

en: The only way to do great work is to love what you do. If you haven't found it yet, keep looking. Don't settle.
zh: 成就伟大工作的唯一方法是热爱你做的事。如果还没找到，继续寻找，不要将就。
author: Steve Jobs

en: It is easier to find men who will volunteer to die, than to find those who are willing to endure pain with patience.
zh: 愿意赴死的人容易找到，愿意耐心承受痛苦的人却很少。
author: Julius Caesar

en: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
zh: 心即是一切。你的所思，即你所成。
author: Buddha

en: We do not stop exercising because we grow old — we grow old because we stop exercising.
zh: 我们不是因为变老而停止运动，而是因为停止运动才变老。
author: Kenneth Cooper

en: Stillness is not the absence of movement, but the presence of peace.
zh: 静，不是没有动，而是有了安宁。

en: Man sacrifices his health in order to make money. Then he sacrifices money to recuperate his health.
zh: 人先牺牲健康去换钱，再牺牲钱来恢复健康。
author: Dalai Lama

en: To a mind that is still the whole universe surrenders.
zh: 心若安定，万物归顺。
author: Zhuangzi

en: The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
zh: 人生最大的荣耀，不在于从不跌倒，而在于每次跌倒后都能站起来。
author: Nelson Mandela

en: If you listen to your body when it whispers, you won't have to hear it scream.
zh: 如果你在身体低语时倾听它，就不必等到它尖叫。

en: Sleep is the best meditation.
zh: 睡眠是最好的冥想。
author: Dalai Lama

en: The art of resting the mind and the power of dismissing from it all care and worry is probably one of the secrets of energy in our great men.
zh: 让心灵休息、将一切忧虑驱散的能力，大概是伟人精力充沛的秘密之一。
author: Captain J. A. Hatfield

en: Happiness is the highest form of health.
zh: 快乐是最高形式的健康。
author: Dalai Lama

en: The soul always knows what to do to heal itself. The challenge is to silence the mind.
zh: 灵魂始终知道如何疗愈自己，挑战在于让心智安静下来。
author: Caroline Myss

en: Half of what I write is garbage. The trick is knowing which half. It's the same with work — half of what keeps you busy doesn't matter.
zh: 我写的东西有一半是垃圾，诀窍在于知道是哪一半。工作也一样——让你忙碌的事情有一半并不重要。
author: Ernest Hemingway

en: When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive — to breathe, to think, to enjoy, to love.
zh: 清晨醒来，想想活着是多么珍贵的特权——能呼吸、能思考、能享受、能去爱。
author: Marcus Aurelius

en: A good laugh and a long sleep are the two best cures for anything.
zh: 开怀大笑和酣然长睡，是治愈一切的两剂良药。
author: Irish Proverb

en: Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our freedom and our power to choose our response.
zh: 在刺激与反应之间有一个空间，那个空间里存在着我们的自由和选择回应的力量。
author: Viktor Frankl

en: I have no special talents. I am only passionately curious.
zh: 我没有什么特殊才能，我只是充满了热情的好奇心。
author: Albert Einstein

en: Do you have the patience to wait until your mud settles and the water is clear?
zh: 你有没有耐心等待，直到你心中的泥沙沉淀、水面变得清澈？
author: Lao Tzu

en: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe.
zh: 与自己和谐相处的人，也与宇宙和谐相处。
author: Marcus Aurelius

en: Your calm mind is the ultimate weapon against your challenges.
zh: 你平静的心，是应对一切挑战的终极武器。
author: Bryant McGill
